paper_title: Variability in Neogloboquadrina pachyderma stable isotope ratios from isothermal conditions: implications for individual foraminifera analysis

methods.description: This method involves measuring stable isotope ratios in Neogloboquadrina pachyderma shells to assess variability within samples and compare with expected equilibrium values. The analysis helps in understanding the implications for individual foraminifera analysis.

steps.description: Stable isotope measurements were performed on groups of four N. pachyderma shells (150-250 µm) with up to six measurements per collection interval.
steps.searchable_summary: perform stable isotope measurements on N. pachyderma shells

steps.description: Remove possible outliers from the dataset to obtain a conservative estimate of variability.
steps.searchable_summary: remove outliers from stable isotope dataset

steps.description: Calculate the residual from the mean for each sample and define outliers as being more than 1.5 times the interquartile range away from the overall mean.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate residuals and identify outliers in stable isotope data

steps.description: Compare the observations to expected δ 18 O equilibrium values and estimates of the δ 13 C of dissolved inorganic carbon (δ 13 C DIC).
steps.searchable_summary: compare stable isotope measurements to expected equilibrium values

steps.description: Calculate equilibrium δ 18 O using the Kim and O'Neil (1997) palaeo-temperature equation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate equilibrium δ 18 O using palaeo-temperature equation

steps.description: Estimate δ 13 C DIC based on multiple-linear regression of temperature, salinity, and nutrients.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate δ 13 C DIC using multiple-linear regression
